item.description: Vice-chancellor of the University of Dhaka Professor ABM Obaidul Islam at an event on Friday urged the graduates of the Institute of Business Administration to become innovators and create new employment opportunities....

item.title: Doctors, academics, activists demand ICU database
item.description: Health rights activists, academics and clinicians at a discussion on Saturday urged the government to bring all intensive care unit facilities under a central database so that patients and healthcare providers can identify where ICU services are available during emergencies....

item.description: A week-long solo art exhibition by interdisciplinary artist Mubtasim Alvee concluded on Saturday at the Alliance Française de Dhaka in Dhanmondi....

item.title: Semenyo's magic moment fires City to FA Cup win
item.description: Antoine Semenyo produced one of the great FA Cup final goals with an audacious back-flick that sealed Manchester City's 1-0 win against Chelsea at Wembley on Saturday....

item.title: Fair share of common river water demanded
item.description: The 50th anniversary of the historic Farakka Long March was celebrated in the country on Saturday with a vow to secure a fair share of water for Bangladesh from the 54 common rivers shared with India....Expat Community Forum

item.title: BMU holds seminar on hypertension in Bangladesh
item.description: The public health and informatics department of Bangladesh Medical University held a seminar titled Hypertension in Bangladesh, marking World Hypertension Day, at the Super Specialised Hospital of the university in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: Experts call for overcoming low-equilibrium tax trap
item.description: Experts on Saturday called for deep structural changes in Bangladesh’s tax system, warning that the country remains stuck in a ‘low-level equilibrium trap’ marked by a narrow tax base, weak governance and limited fiscal space......

item.title: Probe into Premier Bank loan fraud demanded
item.description: Twenty-six export-oriented garment manufacturers on Saturday demanded remedy and investigation into alleged fake loans worth hundreds of crores of taka created in their names by the Narayanganj branch of Premier Bank PLC......

item.title: Bangladesh eyes energy cooperation with Angola
item.description: Bangladesh has expressed strong interest in forging a long-term, government-to-government (G2G) energy cooperation arrangement with Angola and invited an Angolan technical team to visit Dhaka at the earliest opportunity for formal negotiations......Geographic Reference

item.title: BB working to maintain stability in financial sector: governor
item.description: Bangladesh Bank governor Md Mostaqur Rahman on Saturday said the central bank was working to maintain stability in the financial sector with highest priority on controlling inflation......

item.title: Circularity can make Bangladesh a global supply chain hub: Nuria
item.description: Bangladesh has a significant opportunity to become a sustainable global supply chain hub by embracing circular economy principles, attracting green investment, and enhancing export competitiveness......

item.title: DCCI EPI report calls for market price stabilisation
item.description: Speakers at a seminar here today emphasized the need for market price stabilisation and supply chain improvement to protect farmers’ interests and easy and low-interest loans for CMSME entrepreneurs......

item.description: The police on Saturday evening arrested former Begum Rokeya University in Rangpur vice-chancellor Hasibur Rashid who has been convicted and sentenced to 10 years of imprisonment in the Abu Sayeed murder case....

item.description: Farmers and cattle rearers in Sunamganj’s haor region are facing growing uncertainty over livestock feed after heavy rainfall damaged paddy crops and disrupted the collection of straw, the main source of cattle fodder......

item.description: The Bangladesh Jewellers Association has announced a decrease in gold prices in the local market......

item.description: Russian president Vladimir Putin will travel to China on May 19 for a two-day visit, hot on the heels of US President Donald Trump’s trip to Beijing, the Kremlin said on Saturday....

item.description: Global stocks slumped and oil prices rose Friday with worries about sustained inflation driving up bond yields with no conclusion to the Iran war in sight......

item.title: Democrats accuse Trump of stock trade corruption
item.description: Democrats accused US president Donald Trump of corruption on Friday after the disclosure of major stock market transactions carried out in his name, although his son denied there was wrongdoing......

item.description: KNDS said Friday that it planned to push ahead with plans for a public offering of its shares, despite reports Berlin is seeking a delay as it weighs taking a stake in the tank and weapons maker......

item.title: Ties with India depend on Ganges treaty renewal: BNP
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party secretary general and local government minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Saturday said that the establishment of good ties between Bangladesh and India would depend on the successful renewal of the Ganges water sharing treaty....

item.title: Russian economy posts first quarterly decline in 3 years
item.description: The Russian economy shrank by 0.2 percent in the first quarter of 2026, its first quarterly contraction in three years amid mounting strain from the Ukraine war and sanctions, official data showed Friday......

item.title: Ghana ends IMF bailout, eyes new pact
item.description: Ghana has wrapped up its International Monetary Fund bailout programme, officials said Friday, closing a $3 billion support package......

item.title: UAE to ‘accelerate’ new oil pipeline bypassing Hormuz
item.description: The UAE is to fast-track construction of a new oil pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz, official media said on Friday, after the Middle East war crippled exports through the vital waterway......

item.title: China opposes EU probe of security firm Nuctech
item.description: China urged individuals and organisations on Saturday not to cooperate with an anti-subsidy investigation by the European Union into Chinese group Nuctech, which specialises in security screening equipment......

item.title: Russian court orders Euroclear to pay $250b
item.description: A Russian court on Friday ordered Belgian financial group Euroclear to pay around $250 billion in damages over the freezing of billions of dollars worth of Russian assets in the European Union since the Ukraine war......

item.title: Pak minister in Tehran for peace talks
item.description: Pakistan’s interior minister arrived in Tehran on Saturday to help facilitate stalled peace talks between Iran and the United States, as tensions across the Middle East remained high despite a fragile ceasefire and continued Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon....

item.title: Boeing confirms China to buy 200 aircraft
item.description: Aerospace giant Boeing on Friday confirmed that China had committed to purchasing 200 aircraft during a visit to Beijing by US President Donald Trump—a deal that could ultimately balloon with orders for 750 additional planes......

item.title: 56 judges share 28 Dhaka courtrooms
item.description: The High Court monitoring committee for subordinate courts in the Dhaka division expressed concern and dissatisfaction as 56 judges were sharing 28 courtrooms and working hours on a rotational basis in Dhaka courts....Geographic Reference

item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney and the leader of Canada’s oil‑rich Alberta province took a major step Friday toward building an oil pipeline that could substantially increase crude exports to Asia......

item.description: Prime Bank PLC recently signed a strategic agreement with RANCON British Motors Limited (MG) to provide exclusive benefits to its customers on purchasing various MG vehicles......

item.title: Interest payment to rise fourfold in decade
item.description: The projected allocation for the interest payment in the upcoming national budget is likely be Tk 1,42,000 crore -- a fourfold rise in a decade -- putting pressures on allocations for health and education. ...

item.description: Modhumoti Bank PLC organised its 146th meeting of the executive committee on Saturday at the bank’s head office......

item.description: BRAC Bank has successfully arranged a BDT 500 crore non-convertibles, cumulative, fully redeemable preference share facility for United Mymensingh Power Limited, marking the largest redeemable preference share transaction in Bangladesh’s history......Expat Community Forum

item.title: Marcel customer wins Tk10 lakh in digital campaign
item.description: Habibur Rahman, a resident of Pekua Dakshin Para village in Mirzapur, Tangail, has won Tk10 lakh after purchasing a Marcel refrigerator as part of the nationwide ‘Digital Campaign Season – 24.’......

item.title: Qoumi madrassahs continue to remain out of govt radar
item.description: Qoumi madrassahs are mushrooming in the country as repeated government initiatives to bring them under regulation ended in failure, raising various concerns....

item.title: Antidote to fear of maths
item.description: A CHILD sits at a desk, pencil poised over a notebook, staring at a maths problem that feels less like a question and more like a wall. Nearby, a parent watches, wanting to help but feeling the same familiar tug of uncertainty. It is a quiet, shared tension that plays out at kitchen tables in nearly every corner of the world. We have come to accept this struggle as a normal part......

item.title: Revisiting Bangladesh’s Palestine solidarity
item.description: NAKBA, commonly translated as ‘the catastrophe,’ was not the first major incident of Zionist expansionism in historic Palestine — the first Jewish settlements preceded the event by decades— but it was the episode of mass ethnic cleansing that split the history of Palestine and its inhabitants into one of dispossessed subjects. Every year, May 15 is observed as......

item.title: Rethinking seaport financing
item.description: THE port sector is at a critical turning point. Private berth operators have demonstrated unprecedented confidence by submitting a Tk 7,650 crore proposal to renovate Chattogram Port’s ageing general cargo berth. At the same time, exporters and importers are grappling with recent tariff increases imposed by the Chattogram Port Authority, which risk raising the cost of......

item.title: Rejuvenating soil fertility
item.description: SOIL is not only a medium for crop farming. It is a living and dynamic natural resource that sustains food production, environmental balance and rural livelihood. The prosperity of agriculture has for long depended on the fertility of soils, particularly in vast floodplains, terraces, coastal belts and haor ecosystems where millions of farmers cultivate rice and other......

item.title: India’s lethal border policy violates international norms
item.description: THE unabated killing of Bangladeshis at the hands of India’s Border Security Force in no way shows Delhi’s claimed neighbourly policy. Indian border guards killed three Bangladeshis in less than a week. A young man was killed along the Banchawki border in Lalmonirhat on May 14 when he reportedly tried to cross the fence into the Indian territory. On May 9, Indian guards......

item.title: Govt should act fast to save farmers from debt trap
item.description: THE flooding of the haor region spanning seven districts, caused by excessive rainfall in April 27–May 4, has damaged boro crop standing on thousands of hectares of land. The Met Office recorded an average of 605 millimetres of rainfall across the Sylhet division in April, which is more than double the average monthly rainfall of 295 millimetres. Boro, which is the largest......

item.title: Case filed, 3 held as protest at shrine attack on
item.description: The police on Saturday arrested three suspected attackers amid protests of different organisations over the attack on devotees at a shrine in Mirpur area of the capital Dhaka early Friday....Geographic Reference

item.description: A man was arrested on Saturday on charges of strangling his 10-year-old niece to death after raping her at Sirajdikhan upazila in Munshiganj....

item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion arrested an alleged drug trader with 100 kilograms of marijuana in Keraniganj, on the outskirts of Dhaka, early Saturday....

item.description: A daylong cultural event titled ‘Unity in diversity and cultural harmony fair 2026’ was held in Sirajganj on Saturday, highlighting the importance of preserving the languages and cultural heritage of the country’s national minority communities....

item.description: State minister for power, energy and mineral resources and lawmaker, Aninda Islam Amit, on Saturday said that the BNP government has taken initiatives to solve the long standing Bhabadah water stagnation problem permanently....

item.description: Two brothers, Sifat Ahmed, 18, and Swachh Ahmed, 14, drowned in the Teesta River at Rajpur union in Lalmonirhat sadar upazila on Saturday afternoon....

item.description: THE police rescued three students from Khoksa upazila in Kushtia on Friday night, two days after they went missing from Kumarkhali upazila....

item.description: Film activists at a conference on Saturday stated that the development of film culture, education, and the industry requires government initiatives rather than bureaucratic control....

item.description: A Paris magistrate will investigate the 2018 assassination of Saudi dissident journalist Jamal Khashoggi after rights groups filed a complaint against Crown Prince Mohammed Bin Salman, French sources told AFP on Saturday....

item.description: Israel said Saturday it had killed Hamas armed wing chief Ezzedine Al-Haddad in an airstrike in Gaza the previous day, describing him as a key architect of the October 7 attacks....

item.title: Alonso set for appointment as Chelsea manager: reports
item.description:
Xabi Alonso is set to be named as Chelsea's new manager, just four months after being sacked by Real Madrid, according to various media reports on Saturday.

item.title: Govt walks on path of fascism, says Jamaat amir
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir Shafiqur Rahman on Saturday alleged that the government was moving towards becoming another fascistic system by abandoning reform commitments, undermining institutional independence and allowing......Expat Community Forum

item.description: The local administration on Saturday recovered 99 sacks of government relief rice from the possession of a local Jamaat-e-Islami leader at Noakhali’s Subarnachar upazila....

item.description: At least four members of the Bangladesh Ansar and Village Defence Party were injured while trying to prevent a mob attack on a physician at Shariatpur Sadar Hospital in the district early Saturday amid tensions over the death of a patient....

item.description: A patient died at Sher-e-Bangla Medical College Hospital in Barishal after a trolley man allegedly removed her oxygen mask to assist another patient whose family had paid Tk 200 to the hospital staff member....

item.description: Farmers and cattle rearers in Sunamganj’s haor region are facing growing uncertainty over livestock feed after heavy rainfall damaged paddy crops and disrupted the collection of straw, the main source of cattle fodder....

item.description: Most of the advance bus and train tickets have been sold out ahead of Eid-ul-Azha....

item.title: Govt plans to modernise telecom sector, says minister
item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam at a seminar in Dhaka on Saturday said that the government would implement a comprehensive action plan to make the country’s telecom sector modern and investment-friendly....
